headline_drivers: Q1 2026 earnings beat with accelerating demand indicators: revenue $257.7M (+10% YoY, 1.7% above consensus), adjusted EPS $0.81 (+9% YoY, 4–5% above consensus), adjusted EBITDA +14% YoY, reported 2026-05-12

Order momentum and visibility surprised to the upside: bookings up 37% YoY and book-to-bill at 1.10, signaling backlog build and multi-quarter revenue support, highlighted in Q1 2026 results and commentary on 2026-05-12

Guidance reinforced a durable growth story rather than a one-off beat: Q2 2026 revenue guided to $259–$264M (midpoint ~1–2% above Street, implying 6–8% organic growth) and full-year 2026 revenue nudged up to $1.04–$1.055B with EPS $3.50–$3.65 reaffirmed, aligning with or slightly ahead of expectations

Balance-sheet and cash-flow quality reduced perceived risk: strong operating cash flow, free cash flow margin improving to ~18% vs ~12% a year ago, and net cash/negative net debt, called out across post-earnings coverage in mid-May 2026

Technical/flow-through element: the earnings beat-and-raise profile triggered an earnings breakout setup (gap up, 4.1x 10-day average volume, strong close near highs), attracting momentum and breakout traders and extending gains well beyond the initial post-print move
summary: The stock’s sharp 1–4 week move is being driven primarily by Novanta’s Q1 2026 print and guidance: a clean top-line and EPS beat, a 37% YoY surge in bookings with a 1.10 book‑to‑bill, and Q2/full‑year outlook that supports mid‑single‑ to high‑single‑digit organic growth in attractive medical and advanced industrial end markets. These data points told the market demand is re‑accelerating and that the growth runway looks more durable than feared, with improved free cash flow and a solid balance sheet lowering risk. Once that fundamental surprise hit, the name behaved like a textbook earnings breakout—large gap, 4x normal volume, and a strong close—which pulled in momentum capital and extended the move beyond what the raw earnings beat alone would justify. There is no evidence of a specific new mega-customer or regulatory shock; this is a re‑rating on better growth visibility and order momentum, amplified by technical buying.
